title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
inputFormat: Array of integers nums, Integer target
outputFormat: Array of two integers representing the indices

sampleTestCases.explanation: Because nums[0] + nums[1] == 9, we return [0, 1].

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: Find maximum profit from stock prices.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Buy at 1 and sell at 6.

title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array nums, return true if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return false if every element is distinct.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The element 1 occurs twice in the array.

title: Maximum Subarray
description: Given an integer array nums, find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) which has the largest sum and return its sum.

sampleTestCases.explanation: [4,-1,2,1] has the largest sum = 6.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all the elements of nums except nums[i].

sampleTestCases.explanation: The product of elements except 1 is 2*3*4=24. Except 2 is 1*3*4=12.

title: Move Zeroes
description: Given an integer array nums, move all 0's to the end of it while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements.
outputFormat: Array with zeroes moved to the end

sampleTestCases.explanation: All zeros are moved to the back, keeping 1, 3, and 12 in their relative positions.

title: Merge Sorted Array
description: You are given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2, sorted in non-decreasing order, and two integers m and n, representing the number of elements in nums1 and nums2 respectively. Merge them into a single sorted array.
inputFormat: Array nums1, Integer m, Array nums2, Integer n

sampleTestCases.explanation: The arrays we are merging are [1,2,3] and [2,5,6].

title: Majority Element
description: Given an array nums of size n, return the majority element. The majority element is the element that appears more than n / 2 times.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The size of array is 3, and 3 appears twice, which is more than 3/2 times.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise.
constraints: s and t consist of lowercase English letters.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Both strings use the exact same characters with the same frequencies.

title: Reverse String
description: Write a function that reverses a string. The input string is given as an array of characters s. You must do this by modifying the input array in-place.
constraints: s[i] is a printable ascii character.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The array is reversed in place.

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Given a string s, return true if it is a palindrome, considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring cases.
constraints: s consists only of printable ASCII characters.

sampleTestCases.input: "A man, a plan, a canal: Panama"
sampleTestCases.explanation: "amanaplanacanalpanama" is a palindrome.

title: Longest Common Prefix
description: Write a function to find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string.
constraints: strs[i] consists of only lowercase English letters.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The longest prefix shared by all three strings is "fl".

title: Length of Last Word
description: Given a string s consisting of words and spaces, return the length of the last word in the string. A word is a maximal substring consisting of non-space characters only.
constraints: s consists of only English letters and spaces ' '.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The last word is "World" which has a length of 5.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
description: Given a string s, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
constraints: s consists of English letters, digits, symbols and spaces.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The answer is "abc", with the length of 3.

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is the range [0, 5000].
outputFormat: Head of the reversed linked list

sampleTestCases.explanation: The list structure is inverted so that the tail becomes the new head.

title: Middle of Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the middle node of the linked list. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [1, 100].

sampleTestCases.explanation: The middle node of the list is node 3.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2. Merge the two lists in a one sorted list.
constraints: The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50].
inputFormat: Heads of two sorted linked lists
outputFormat: Head of the merged sorted linked list

sampleTestCases.explanation: Nodes from both lists are spliced together in sorted order.

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Given head, the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 10^4].

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a cycle in the linked list, where the tail connects to the 1st node (0-indexed).

title: Remove Nth Node From End
description: Given the head of a linked list, remove the nth node from the end of the list and return its head.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is sz.
inputFormat: Head of a linked list, Integer n
outputFormat: Head of the modified linked list

sampleTestCases.explanation: The second node from the end is 4, which is removed.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All brackets are closed correctly in the correct order.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
constraints: Methods pop, top and getMin will always be called on non-empty stacks.
At most 3 * 10^4 calls will be made to push, pop, top, and getMin.

sampleTestCases.explanation: getMin() returns -3, after popping top element, getMin() returns -2.

title: Daily Temperatures
description: Given an array of integers temperatures represents the daily temperatures, return an array answer such that answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait after the i-th day to get a warmer temperature.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For 75, the next warmer temp is 76, which is 4 days away.

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description: Evaluate the value of an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation (Postfix Expression). Valid operators are +, -, *, and /.
constraints: tokens[i] is either a valid operator or an integer in the range [-200, 200].

title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return its maximum depth. A binary tree's maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root node down to the farthest leaf node.
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4].
inputFormat: Root node of a binary tree

sampleTestCases.explanation: The maximum path goes from 3 -> 20 -> 15 (or 7), which has a length of 3 nodes.

title: Invert Binary Tree
description: Given the root of a binary tree, invert the tree, and return its root.
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100].
inputFormat: Root node of a binary tree
outputFormat: Root of the inverted binary tree

sampleTestCases.explanation: Every left and right child node connection is swapped recursively.

title: Same Tree
description: Given the roots of two binary trees p and q, write a function to check if they are the same or not. Two binary trees are considered the same if they are structurally identical, and the nodes have the same value.
constraints: The number of nodes in both trees is in the range [0, 100].
inputFormat: Roots of two binary trees p and q

sampleTestCases.explanation: Both trees are identical in structure and values.

title: Level Order Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the level order traversal of its nodes' values. (i.e., from left to right, level by level).
constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000].
inputFormat: Root node of a binary tree
outputFormat: List of lists containing integer node values level by level

sampleTestCases.explanation: Values grouped by their level depths from top to bottom.

title: Binary Search
description: Given an array of integers nums which is sorted in ascending order, and an integer target, write a function to search target in nums. If target exists, then return its index. Otherwise, return -1.
constraints: All the integers in nums are unique.
nums is sorted in ascending order.
inputFormat: Array of sorted integers nums, Integer target

sampleTestCases.explanation: 9 exists in nums and its index is 4.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can either climb 1 or 2 steps. In how many distinct ways can you climb to the top?

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are two ways to climb to the top: 1 step + 1 step, or 2 steps.

title: House Robber
description: You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. Each house has a certain amount of money stashed. You cannot rob adjacent houses. Return the maximum amount of money you can rob tonight without alerting the police.
inputFormat: Array of integers nums representing money
outputFormat: Integer maximum money that can be robbed

sampleTestCases.explanation: Rob house 1 (money = 1) and then rob house 3 (money = 3). Total amount = 1 + 3 = 4.
